author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
title: The Same Continued.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Bishop John narrated all this in the hearing of Pelagius; but he, of course, might respectfully say: “Your holiness is in error; you do not accurately remember the facts.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: It was not in reference to the passages of Scripture which you have quoted that I uttered the words: ‘This is what I also believe.’

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Because this is not my opinion of them.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: I do not understand them to say, that God’s grace so co-operates with man, that his abstinence from sin is due, not to ‘him that willeth, nor to him that runneth, but to God that showeth mercy.’”
